scenario: Helios Fintech wires a JobWorkerMetrics implementation into a Camunda 8.8 Java job worker that processes 4800 transactions per day. The developer uses the built in Micrometer adapter with a Prometheus MeterRegistry and needs the correct builder call to attach the metrics instance to the specific worker being opened.
question: Which builder call attaches a JobWorkerMetrics implementation to a Camunda 8.8 Java job worker on the Java client?

options.text: Pass the JobWorkerMetrics instance to CamundaClientBuilder using .metrics(metrics) so the metrics instance applies globally to every job worker opened by the configured Camunda client at Helios Fintech

Register the JobWorkerMetrics instance with the gateway through client.registerMetrics(metrics) so the broker tags activate jobs responses sent to this Java client at Helios Fintech with the metrics instance

Wrap the JobHandler with a MetricsJobHandler decorator and the Java client at Helios Fintech automatically discovers the JobWorkerMetrics implementation through a JobHandler annotation on the wrapped handler

Pass the JobWorkerMetrics instance to the JobWorkerBuilder via .metrics(metrics) on client.newJobWorker so the worker at Helios Fintech reports its activated and handled job counts to the supplied metrics instance

a.text: Incorrect. JobWorkerMetrics is attached per worker on the JobWorkerBuilder via .metrics(metrics); the CamundaClientBuilder has no global metrics method that applies to every worker.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no client.registerMetrics API and the broker does not tag responses with client metrics; the worker reports its own counters via the JobWorkerMetrics passed to the builder.

c.text: Incorrect. The Java client does not discover metrics through a JobHandler annotation or a decorator class; the JobWorkerMetrics instance is supplied explicitly via the builder.

d.text: Correct. The Usage example shows client.newJobWorker().jobType(...).handler(handler).metrics(metrics).open(); the .metrics(metrics) call on the JobWorkerBuilder attaches the JobWorkerMetrics implementation to the worker.

explanation: The Camunda 8.8 Java client attaches a JobWorkerMetrics implementation per worker via .metrics(metrics) on the JobWorkerBuilder returned from client.newJobWorker(). The optional built in Micrometer implementation is constructed with JobWorkerMetrics.micrometer().withMeterRegistry(...).build().
